mission.title: modelpack: ci should not pass when new fields are added in GO structs but json schema is not corresponding changed
mission.description: ci should not pass when new fields are added in GO structs but json schema is not corresponding changed. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current modelpack deployment
steps.description:
Review the modelpack model-spec repository and its CI configuration:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec.git
cd model-spec
cat Makefile
```
This issue is about CI validation for Go struct to JSON schema consistency.

steps.title: Review modelpack configuration
steps.description:
Review how the ModelConfig Go struct relates to the JSON schema:
```bash
# Check the Go struct definition
find . -name '*.go' | xargs grep 'type ModelConfig struct'

# Check the JSON schema
find . -name '*.json' | head -10
```
Currently, when a contributor adds a new field to ModelConfig but forgets to update the JSON schema, CI does not catch the mismatch.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ci should not pass when new fields are added in GO structs…
steps.description:
The fix adds CI validation to detect mismatches between Go struct fields and JSON schema definitions.

The implementation validates whether examples in `config.md` contain any unknown fields that are not in the JSON schema, providing an indirect check for schema completeness.

See the fix PR for details: https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec/pull/100

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Verify the CI check works by running the validation locally:
```bash
# Run the schema validation
make validate

# Or run Go tests
go test ./...
```
Confirm that adding a new Go struct field without updating the JSON schema causes the CI check to fail.

resolution.summary: This PR originally aimed to enforce validation of unknown fields in ModelConfig during CI. Specifically, if a new field is added to ModelConfig without corresponding changes in the JSON schema, the CI would fail, prompting the contributor to update the JSON schema accordingly. However, the implementation will bring significant complexity to the code base (I cannot find an easy to acheive this).

prerequisites.description: Go development environment with the model-spec repository cloned.
